refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated capability to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints), record derived requirements with traceability to source inputs, and maintain a managed baseline history including calibration updates and performance target revisions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct functional concerns: derivation (analysis/synthesis), recording (data capture), and management (lifecycle control). Refinement separates these into analyzable sub-functions while maintaining traceability to worksite inputs and baseline management as acceptance criteria. High safety criticality demands explicit linkage to safety constraints in derivation logic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall demonstrate: (1) a documented derivation method that maps worksite inputs (site conditions, task type, safety plan) to performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability); (2) a recorded baseline with full traceability chain from worksite context to derived requirement; (3) a managed history showing at least two calibration/update cycles with audit trail.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context inputs (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints) using a defined methodology.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs and acceptance criteria.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed baseline history of performance targets, calibration updates, and requirement revisions with audit trail.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite context (via Tasking interface), applies a rule-based derivation model, outputs performance targets, and logs all derivations to a persistent baseline registry with version control.
DP-B: Distributed derivation where each Vehicle module computes local performance targets based on received Tasking, with periodic synchronization to a central baseline manager for history and audit.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement specification by Site Manager with system-assisted validation against worksite constraints, recorded in a configuration management system with change control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite context (via Tasking interface), applies a rule-based derivation model, outputs performance targets, and logs all derivations to a persistent baseline registry with version control.

potential_sources: Coupling to Tasking interface design (must convey sufficient worksite context for derivation).
Coupling to Safety Services for constraint injection (safety plan inputs must be available to derivation logic).
Coupling to Vehicle telemetry feedback loop (actual performance vs. derived targets for calibration).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling within the performance derivation domain itself (rule-based model is self-contained) but requires well-defined external interfaces for context input and constraint integration. Decoupling is achieved by treating Tasking and SafetyAlert as contract-based inputs rather than direct dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite context (site conditions, task type, safety constraints) can be reliably captured and transmitted via Tasking interface.
Assumption: A rule-based derivation model can be defined and validated to produce performance targets within acceptable accuracy.
Risk: If worksite context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived targets may be invalid; mitigation requires validation gate and operator override capability.
Risk: Derivation model maintenance and updates may become a bottleneck; mitigation requires clear ownership and change control process.
Risk: High safety criticality demands formal verification of derivation logic; standard testing may be insufficient.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the derivation model specification (rule set, algorithms, constraints) as a separate design artifact. Define acceptance criteria for worksite context completeness. Establish a formal verification/validation plan for safety-critical derivation logic.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end performance requirement derivation, recording, and baseline management capability. It integrates worksite context inputs (via Tasking), applies the derivation model, maintains the baseline registry, and exposes performance targets and history to other system modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, operational constraints) from Site Manager or Tele-operator. Used as primary input to performance derivation logic.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services. Integrated into derivation model to ensure derived performance targets respect safety plan and operational policies.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives actual performance data (cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Used for calibration, baseline validation, and historical comparison.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides persistent storage and synchronization of baseline registry, performance history, and audit trail. Enables remote access by Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment: Performance derivation, recording, and baseline management are core system-level functions. NGHE Solution is the natural owner of end-to-end requirement lifecycle. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces are well-defined contract points (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link).

Low containment: Vehicle is a physical asset focused on task execution, not requirement derivation. If performance derivation were distributed to Vehicle, it would require embedding analytics logic in each vehicle instance, creating high diffusion and maintenance burden. Vehicle should consume derived targets, not generate them.

Low containment: Teleoperation is a control/interface module for remote operation. Performance requirement derivation is orthogonal to tele-operation control logic. High diffusion risk if derivation logic is embedded in teleoperation stack.

Moderate-low containment: Safety Services provides constraints and alerts but does not own the derivation process itself. Performance requirement derivation is a broader system function that consumes safety constraints as inputs. Ownership by Safety Services would create tight coupling and limit flexibility in derivation methodology.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) performance requirement derivation is a system-level function that integrates inputs from multiple domains (worksite context, safety, operational constraints); (2) the baseline registry and history management are system-wide assets; (3) containment within NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and maintains a single source of truth for performance targets; (4) external interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined contract points that decouple derivation logic from implementation details of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution module becomes a monolithic owner of too many system functions, it may become a bottleneck for development and testing. Mitigation: Define clear sub-module boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Derivation Engine, Baseline Registry, Audit Trail) with internal interfaces.

Risk: Dependency on Tasking interface completeness and accuracy. If worksite context is not reliably transmitted, derivation quality suffers. Mitigation: Establish validation gates and operator override capability.

Risk: Integration with Safety Services (SafetyAlert input) requires tight coordination. Changes to safety constraints must propagate to derivation logic. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert contract clearly and establish change control process.

Risk: Telemetry feedback loop for calibration requires Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to report actual performance. If telemetry is incomplete or delayed, baseline validation is compromised. Mitigation: Define telemetry requirements and SLAs.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish internal sub-module structure within NGHE Solution to separate Derivation Engine, Baseline Registry, and Audit Trail functions. Define clear ownership and interface contracts for each sub-module. Establish SLAs for Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry inputs to ensure derivation quality.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (centralized analytics engine with rule-based derivation and persistent baseline registry) directly addresses all three sub-FRs: (1) derivation via rule-based model applied to worksite context; (2) recording via baseline registry with traceability; (3) management via version control and audit trail. The DP is logically sound and decoupled from implementation details. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion risk: If derivation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, it may be replicated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, creating maintenance burden and inconsistency. Mitigation: Enforce single ownership in NGHE Solution.

Diffusion risk: Baseline registry and audit trail must be centralized and accessible to all stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality). If distributed across modules, synchronization and consistency become problematic. Mitigation: Centralize in NGHE Solution with Network Link for persistence.

Diffusion risk: SafetyAlert integration requires coordination between NGHE Solution and Safety Services. If safety constraints are not consistently applied to derivation, safety criticality is compromised. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert contract and establish change control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution provides optimal containment with minimal diffusion. All derivation, recording, and management logic is owned by a single module. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined contract points. No evidence of diffusion across Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via: (1) unit testing of derivation model against test cases derived from worksite scenarios; (2) integration testing of Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry inputs; (3) baseline registry validation (traceability, history, audit trail); (4) end-to-end scenario testing with Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders. High safety criticality requires formal verification of derivation logic (e.g., model checking, theorem proving) in addition to standard testing.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns CR-NGHE-005 and its three sub-FRs. Interfaces to Vehicle (Telemetry), Teleoperation (Telemetry), Safety Services (SafetyAlert), and Site Manager (Tasking, Network Link) are explicit. No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Establish formal specification of the derivation model (rule set, algorithms, constraints) as a separate design artifact. This is critical for safety-critical verification.
Define acceptance criteria for worksite context completeness and accuracy (Tasking interface). Specify validation gates and operator override capability.
Establish SLAs for Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry inputs to ensure derivation quality and timeliness.
Define sub-module structure within NGHE Solution (Derivation Engine, Baseline Registry, Audit Trail) with internal interfaces to manage complexity.
Establish change control process for derivation model updates and safety constraint changes (SafetyAlert).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 has been successfully refined into three atomic sub-FRs (derivation, recording, management) with a coherent DP (centralized analytics engine) and clear IM assignment (NGHE Solution). All gates pass. However, the sub-FRs are not yet atomic enough for detailed design. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to address implementation details (e.g., derivation model specification, baseline registry schema, audit trail format). Zigzag down to decompose each sub-FR into lower-level functional and design details.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1.1: Define and validate the performance derivation model (rule set, algorithms, constraint handling) that maps worksite context to performance targets.
CR-NGHE-005.1.2: Integrate safety constraints (SafetyAlert) into the derivation model to ensure derived targets respect safety plan and operational policies.
CR-NGHE-005.2.1: Design and implement the baseline registry schema to record derived performance requirements with full traceability to source worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.2.2: Establish traceability links from derived performance targets back to worksite mission context (site conditions, task objectives, safety constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.3.1: Implement version control and change management for performance target baselines.
CR-NGHE-005.3.2: Implement audit trail logging for all derivation, recording, and management operations with timestamp and operator identification.

assumptions: Worksite context (site conditions, task type, safety constraints) can be reliably captured and transmitted via Tasking interface.
A rule-based derivation model can be defined and validated to produce performance targets within acceptable accuracy.
Safety constraints from Safety Services (SafetyAlert) are available and can be integrated into derivation logic.
Actual performance data (Telemetry) is available from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for calibration and validation.
Persistent storage (Network Link) is available for baseline registry and audit trail.
Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality, and other stakeholders have access to baseline history and audit trail via Network Link.
open_risks: Derivation model specification and validation: High safety criticality demands formal verification; standard testing may be insufficient. Recommend formal methods (model checking, theorem proving) for safety-critical logic.
Worksite context completeness: If Tasking interface does not convey sufficient context, derived targets may be invalid. Recommend validation gates and operator override capability.
SafetyAlert integration: Changes to safety constraints must propagate to derivation logic. Recommend clear change control process.
Telemetry feedback loop: If actual performance data is incomplete or delayed, baseline validation is compromised. Recommend SLAs for telemetry reporting.
Scalability: If derivation model becomes complex (many rules, constraints, scenarios), performance and maintainability may suffer. Recommend modular design and performance testing.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of the derivation model (rule set, algorithms, constraint handling).
Validation test cases derived from representative worksite scenarios.
Formal verification results (model checking, theorem proving) for safety-critical derivation logic.
Baseline registry schema design with traceability requirements.
Version control and audit trail implementation design.
Integration test plan for Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry interfaces.
Acceptance criteria for worksite context completeness and accuracy.
SLAs for Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Telemetry inputs.
Change control process for derivation model updates and safety constraint changes.
